en: If your company performs any of the following services and is required to maintain the relevant certification(s) for those services, you will be asked to provide your certificate in a subsequent form.
Japan High-Pressure Gas License Applicability Assessment

The High-pressure Gas License is mandated by the High-Pressure Gas Safety Act (Act No. 204 of 1951), which regulates the manufacture, storage, sale, transport, and use of high-pressure gases in Japan to prevent disasters and protect public safety. The law explicitly requires licenses or notices depending on the kind and scale of high-pressure gas activity, per Article 1 and Article 3 of the Act.

Administrative Procedures of the High-Pressure Gas Safety Act
Does your company manufacture, store, sell, or transport high-pressure gases above regulated volumes or pressures in Japan and is required to maintain a High-pressure Gas License?
By providing your electronic signature, you confirm that the information submitted in this questionnaire is true, complete, and accurate in accordance with Japanese legislation.
Please explain why your company is exempt from needing a High-pressure Gas License.
More information on the specific exemptions under the Enforcement Decree of the High Pressure Gas Safety Act can be found here.
Please select from the following reasons your company is exempt from needing a High-pressure Gas License.
The company does not manufacture, store, sell, or transport high-pressure gases above regulated volumes or pressures.

The company manufactures, stores, sells, or transports only liquefied gas with a volume of 1 liter or less, and the pressure is 0.8 megapascals or less at a temperature of 35°C (2.1 megapascals or less in the case of fluorocarbons (excluding flammable gases)) as determined by the Minister of Economy, Trade and Industry.
